ogTitle: Taylors Beach vs Tea Gardens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Taylors Beach and Tea Gardens.
heading: Taylors Beach vs Tea Gardens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Taylors Beach, New South Wales and Tea Gardens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tea Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Taylors Beach (51/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Taylors Beach or Tea Gardens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Taylors Beach has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Tea Gardens scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Taylors Beach scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Tea Gardens scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Taylors Beach or Tea Gardens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tea Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
